behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real STT model diarization output (speaker labels), which a stub would freeze and potentially hide failures. Evidence: behavior explicitly requires "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels". Boundary signal: this is model output quality, not just transport/retry wiring. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: an Integration test with mocked STT can always return perfect labels and miss real diarization errors. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one model-output property, not a multi-agentic sequence assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio sample with known speaker turns, call the real STT API with diarization enabled, and assert speaker labels remain correct through the transcript object consumed by summarization (e.g., sampled turn-to-speaker checks).

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is parser/transform logic on transcript text and schema shaping, with no required OS/network/subprocess or real model judgment. Rejected Integration: behavior text focuses on "parsing extracts ... into structure" (single logic boundary), not cross-module state propagation failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parsing function with transcript fixtures containing explicit assignments (including edge phrasing) and assert extracted records contain correct `assignee` and `task_text` fields in the summary input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: This checks LLM output completeness/coverage over attendees, which depends on real model decisions. Evidence: "live summary output" and per-attendee action-item coverage requirement. Boundary signal: pass/fail hinges on what the model includes, not only orchestration. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: Integration with fixed canned summary can pass while real model omits attendees. Rejected Workflow: this behavior validates one final summarization outcome criterion, not a required multi-step agentic chain assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Run a transcript with known assignments per attendee through the real summarization model and assert the produced action-items list includes at least one item mapped to each assigned attendee.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: The behavior text says "identified decisions" then "routes ... into schema", indicating deterministic routing/mapping logic after identification. No real infra or model-output quality is required. Rejected Agentic: behavior does not ask whether model identifies decisions correctly, only where already-identified items are placed.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the routing function with input containing pre-identified decision objects and narrative text, then assert decision objects populate `decisions` schema fields and are excluded from generic narrative-only fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This requires real LLM extraction/placement quality from transcript content into structured sections. Evidence: "Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's decisions section". Boundary signal: model judgment about salience/section placement drives correctness. Adjacent-lower miss-proof: mocked summaries can place decisions correctly by fixture and hide real model misses/misplacement. Rejected Workflow: single-output quality check; no explicit requirement for multiple agentic handoff stages.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a transcript containing explicit, verifiable decisions to the live summarization model and assert those decisions are present in `decisions` and not only present in free-form narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a sequence of multiple agentic steps (segment STT/summarization plus merge) where correctness depends on cross-step handoff and final coherence. Integration brake: (b) yes, real AI outputs matter; (c) yes, multiple agentic steps are chained; therefore upgrade to Workflow. Rejected Agentic: behavior text requires sequential segmentation + merge orchestration across steps, not one model decision.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through production segmentation flow, verify sequential segment processing occurred, then assert merged final summary preserves cross-segment continuity (action items/decisions/attributions remain coherent and non-duplicative).

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end orchestration across real upload/delivery infrastructure and multiple agentic stages (transcribe then summarize). Failures can emerge only in full chained execution with real dependencies. Rejected System: behavior explicitly includes AI stages and full pipeline sequencing, so System alone is too low.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting recording in a staging/prod-like environment: upload audio, wait for transcription and summarization, and assert structured output is delivered automatically with expected sections and no human retry/manual patching.
